body.p:
First steps in XQuery work very nicely with the Query Console on the MarkLogic Server (port 8000). If you also have the oXygen Editor open in parallel, you can put the test results from the console directly into an XQuery server script.
UWE test footnote (36/40, callout J): DITA fn maps to UWE footnote/key/desc.
UWE supplement (fn-add) for the footnote index.
body.section:
Loops
You can get the first 10 documents on the server, for example, with:Programming ConstructsLoops - for
(doc())[position() lt 11]
The numbers from 1 to 100 with: (for $i in (1 to 100) return $i)
If you write these two statements one below the other in the console, you get an error. If you separate them with a comma — creating a tuple — then it works. | | Rejected by , . | | Rejected by , .

Creating Documents in the DB
The command for creating a document in the MarkLogic DB looks as follows (docs) xdmp:document-insert:Programming ConstructsDB insert
xdmp:document-insert( "/alex-test/example-alex.xml", <root>Hier steht der Content</root>, <options xmlns="xdmp:document-insert"> <metadata>{ map:map() => map:with("valid-start", "2014-06-03T14:13:05.472585-07:00") => map:with("valid-end", "9999-12-31T11:59:59Z") }</metadata> </options>)
If you put this instruction inside the for loop above, the construct looks like this: (for $i in (1 to 10) return xdmp:document-insert( concat("/alex-test/example-alex-",$i,".xml"), <root>Hier steht der Content {$i}</root>, <options xmlns="xdmp:document-insert"> <metadata>{ map:map() => map:with("valid-start", "2014-06-03T14:13:05.472585-07:00") => map:with("valid-end", "9999-12-31T11:59:59Z") }</metadata> </options>) )
Documents can be assigned to a collectionProgramming ConstructsDB Collection to make them easier to find and evaluate. This is done with the following command (docs) xdmp:document-add-collections and this snippet: let $root := <mein-test> <id>{$id}</id> <content>Hallo Welt!</content> </mein-test>, $options := <options xmlns="xdmp:document-insert"> <permissions>{xdmp:default-permissions()}</permissions> <collections> <collection>/alex-test</collection> </collections> </options>, $fname := concat('/',$id,'_','.xml'), $td := xdmp:document-insert($fname,$root,$options) return [...]
The documents tagged with the collection alex-test can be listed with the following loop: | | Rejected by , . | | Rejected by , . for $x in collection("/alex-test") return fn:document-uri($x)
